name: Sourdough Starter (Buttermilk)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all ingredients in a glass bowl.
2. Cover tightly with plastic wrap.
3. Set in the warm part of your kitchen for 3-5 days.
Storage:
1. Starter can be stored in the refrigerator and must be used within 1 week or refreshed.
2. Use a "crock" from a "cold-packed cheese" to store, covering tightly.
Proving:
1. Bring starter to room temperature.
2. In a glass bowl, combine all of the starter with 1 1/2 cups of flour and 1 cup of water.
3. Cover bowl with plastic wrap and set in a warm place for 12 hours.
Usage:
1. Measure out 2 cups of "proofed" starter and set aside.
2. Return remaining starter to its "crock" and refrigerate until needed again.
Note: This starter improves over time, so it's best to use it at least once a week or refresh it.
